purpose: Day-6 RETROSPECTIVE external validation of the calibrated GO/WITHHOLD gate against INDEPENDENT real-world therapeutic ground truth NOT constructed by the pipeline.
honesty_contract: External truth pre-registered and hash-frozen BEFORE scoring. Negative/marginal results reported as such. No tuning to cross a threshold. All labels traceable to Open Targets drug rows.

prereg.purpose: Day-6 RETROSPECTIVE external validation: pre-registration of the INDEPENDENT ground-truth label set, written BEFORE any label fetch or scoring.
prereg.claim_under_test: The calibrated gate's GO/WITHHOLD ranking predicts which scored genes are backed by INDEPENDENT real-world therapeutic evidence (approved drugs / clinical-trial targets for autoimmune indications) BETTER than function-blind baselines (random, OT-association-alone, eQTL-count-alone).
prereg.why_external: The pipeline's own Check-A rule was built from {immune-GO, OT_T1D>T2D, GWAS-T2D-tag, measured Treg direction}. It NEVER used drug approval or clinical-trial precedent. Drug/clinical-precedent labels are therefore not constructed by this project's rule => a validation that can fail.
prereg.external_truth_source: Open Targets Platform Disease.drugAndClinicalCandidates (known + investigational drugs) for a FIXED, pre-registered set of autoimmune / immune-mediated indications. A scored gene is labelled POSITIVE iff it is the mechanistic target of >=1 such drug meeting the tier threshold.

tier_definitions.tier2_PRIMARY_any_clinical: target of a drug at ANY clinical stage (maxClinicalStage >= 1, i.e. Phase I and above, incl. Approved) for any pre-registered autoimmune indication
tier_definitions.tier1_STRICT_approved_or_late: target of a drug that is Approved OR Phase III+ (maxClinicalStage >= 3) for any pre-registered autoimmune indication

prereg.scoring_set: All 44 genes that received a gate GO/WITHHOLD call in day0_checkA_enriched_execution_receipt.json (sha256 25081a891594cbe29d11e2e63dec6dc9c8758074df3241a3b6ce9e49ee730bd1). Includes the trio CD226/RASGRP1/PRKCQ.
prereg.sensitivity_set: 35-gene subset excluding the 9 pure-metabolic NOGO-arm distractors (ABCC8,GIPR,HNF4A,IRS1,JAZF1,NOTCH2,PDX1,PPARG,THADA), reported as robustness.

predictors_compared.gate: binary claude_go (GO=1/WITHHOLD=0) from the calibrated gate/adjudication
predictors_compared.ot_alone: OT T1D association score (ot_t1d_assoc) - function-blind continuous baseline
predictors_compared.eqtl_alone: eQTL n_significant count - function-blind continuous baseline

prereg.metrics: AUROC (primary) and average precision; bootstrap 95% CI (2000 resamples); DeLong/permutation for gate-vs-OT. Small n acknowledged.

decision_rule.yes: gate AUROC point estimate > best baseline AND gate-vs-best-baseline advantage 95% CI excludes 0
decision_rule.marginal: gate AUROC > best baseline but CI includes 0
decision_rule.underpowered: n_external_positives < 4 OR bootstrap CI so wide no ranking is distinguishable

prereg.honesty_note: OT association score is PARTLY informed by the same known-drug datasource, making ot_alone a DELIBERATELY STRONG (conservative) baseline. If the binary gate ties/loses to OT-alone we report that honestly. Labels are frozen with a hash before any AUROC is computed. A NEGATIVE result will be reported as NEGATIVE.

external_truth_source: Open Targets Platform — Target.drugAndClinicalCandidates (known + investigational drugs), intersected with a pre-registered set of 15 autoimmune/immune-mediated indications. A gene is POSITIVE iff it is the mechanistic target of >=1 drug at >=Phase I for such an indication (tier2). Independent of the pipeline's rule (which used immune-GO, OT-T1D>T2D, GWAS-T2D-tag, measured Treg direction — never drug/clinical precedent).
predictors: gate=binary claude_go (GO=1/WITHHOLD=0); ot_alone=OT T1D assoc score; eqtl_alone=eQTL n_sig count; random=uniform (analytic 0.5)
honest_verdict: Against INDEPENDENT real-world drug-target truth (Open Targets autoimmune known/investigational drug targets), the calibrated gate ranks the externally-validated targets well above every function-blind baseline: gate AUROC=0.75 vs eQTL-alone 0.56, random 0.50, and OT-association-alone 0.36 (which is actively worse than chance because OT-T1D score is dominated by metabolic/beta-cell genes that are NOT T-cell drug targets). The gate beats the STRONGEST baseline with permutation p=0.036, and beats OT-alone with a bootstrap advantage CI that excludes 0 [+0.18,+0.59]. However the gate-minus-best-baseline bootstrap CI barely includes 0 (lower bound -0.003), and the strict Approved/Phase-III+ tier has only 2 positives => underpowered. Per the pre-registered decision rule this is MARGINAL, not a clean win. The direction is real and consistent (point estimate + permutation test both favour the gate; excluding metabolic distractors raises the gate to AUROC 0.81), but n=5 external positives is a genuinely small sample and the CI is honest about that. This is external corroboration of the gate's ranking that the pipeline did NOT construct — a step above the self-concordance plateau — reported as a marginal-positive PILOT, not overclaimed.
